title: #51/#52/#48 — the session-start cost is in the instructions, not the hooks; plus the close's missing commit step
action: Read CONTEXT.md D5/D6/D7 first. MEASURED, do not re-measure: the automatic layer is CHEAP — session-init ~95-100ms emitting ~5.4k chars (~1.4k tokens), the per-prompt hook ~50ms emitting 0 bytes when deduped. The cost is what the INSTRUCTIONS then demand before the agent has read the user's question: AGENTS.md (18KB) + bee-hive/SKILL.md (33KB) + references/routing-and-contracts.md (34KB) + docs/knowledge/index.md (13KB) plus a 20k-token knowledge budget — and `status --json` (422ms) and `decisions active --recent 3` largely RE-FETCH what the preamble just injected. (#51, D5) NOTHING executes the verify command automatically — grep proves only two call sites, both in worktree merge. The real defect is a CONDITIONAL rule rendered inside an UNCONDITIONAL numbered list: `AGENTS.md` `## Startup` item 7 reads 'Baseline gate: if .bee/config.json records commands.verify, run it once per session BEFORE CLAIMING ANY CELL' while items 1, 3 and 6 of the same list say 'every session'. An agent working the list top-to-bottom runs a 30-90s chain on a question that touches no cell. FIX: (a) LEAD WITH THE TRIGGER — reword to 'Before your first `cells claim`, run the verify command once...' everywhere it appears: `AGENTS.md`, `skills/bee-hive/templates/AGENTS.block.md` (keep byte-consistent), the preamble line in `skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/inject.mjs`, and `skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md`; (b) move it OUT of the `## Startup` numbered list into the execution section so structure and meaning agree. (#52, D6) Say plainly, where the scout is specified (`AGENTS.md` steps 3/6 and `bee-hive/SKILL.md` §Session Scout), that THE PREAMBLE ALREADY CARRIES phase, gates, feature, cell counts, PBI counts and the recent critical patterns — so `status --json` and `decisions active` are re-run only when about to ROUTE WORK, not to answer a question. Do not delete the calls; make them conditional on intent and say which surface already answered them. Do NOT attempt to shrink the skills themselves — out of scope. (#48, D7-adjacent) Nothing writes the close phase in the wrong ORDER — the guard is correctly locked and gated — but no commit step exists anywhere in the close chain, so `compounding-complete` is set with the learnings file, decisions, backlog rows and specs sitting uncommitted. Add the commit as an explicit step in `skills/bee-compounding/SKILL.md` between the scratch sweep and the state update, and state that the phase is set AFTER the commit lands. (#53, D7) NO CODE CHANGE — bee has no mechanism that surfaces scratch scripts, and its own write guard forces helper scripts into the scratch home. But the adjacent finding IS real and in scope: the scratch home currently holds ~75 stale entries including ~20 executable scripts, so the documented per-feature sweep is not actually running at close. Investigate why `bee tmp sweep --feature <f>` leaves them (is it only sweeping `<scratch>/<feature>/` while agents write to the scratch ROOT?) and fix so the documented behavior is the real behavior. D9 — THIS CELL OWNS THE DERIVED-ARTIFACT REGEN FOR THE WHOLE FEATURE, after i-1 and i-2 have landed: runtime mirror copy, `render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs`, `release_manifest.mjs --write`, `onboard_bee.mjs --repo-root . --apply`. Full chain BOTH ways as the LAST action.

truths: the baseline gate leads with its trigger and no longer sits inside the unconditional Startup list, in ALL FOUR places it appears — AGENTS.md, AGENTS.block.md, inject.mjs, bee-hive/SKILL.md — and AGENTS.md stays byte-consistent with its template
the scout spec names the preamble as the surface that already answered phase/gates/feature/patterns, and makes the re-fetch conditional on routing work
bee-compounding carries an explicit commit step before the state update
the scratch sweep is shown clearing what agents actually write — reproduce the stale case, then show it swept
the instruction fence stays green (these are instruction surfaces it governs)
node scripts/run_verify.mjs exits 0 AND BEE_AGENT_NAME=w-test node scripts/run_verify.mjs exits 0, after all four regens
prohibitions: never weaken the baseline gate itself — a red baseline must still block building on red; only its trigger wording and placement change
do not delete the scout's status/decisions calls — make them conditional and say what already answered them
do not try to shrink AGENTS.md or the skills — out of scope (D6)
no code change for #53's reported symptom — only the stale-scratch finding is in scope

trace.outcome: The session-start cost was never in the hooks — measured, they are ~150ms and ~1.4k tokens. It was a conditional rule rendered inside an unconditional checklist: the baseline gate said 'before claiming any cell' as item 7 of a list whose other items said 'every session', so an agent working top-to-bottom spent 30-90s on questions that touch no cell. It now leads with its trigger in all four places and lives in the execution section, with its strength untouched. The scout now names the preamble as the surface that already answered phase, gates, feature, counts, patterns and decisions, and re-fetches only when about to route work — answering a question is not routing work. The close gained the commit step it never had, with the phase set after it lands. And the scratch sweeper turned out to be bee contradicting itself: its own write guard sends agents to write into the scratch ROOT, while the sweeper's lister saw only directories — so every file written where the guard sent it was unsweepable by every flag including --all. Fixed with the containment proof untouched, plus a guarded prefix rule so the per-feature command the close documents actually reaches that feature's artifacts.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "red_failure_evidence": "Three separate before-states, each measured on the pre-change tree. (#51) The baseline gate sat as item 7 of AGENTS.md's `## Startup` list, whose items 1, 3 and 6 read 'every session', while item 7 itself said 'before claiming any cell' — a conditional rule rendered inside an unconditional checklist, costing a 30-90s chain on any question that touches no cell. Triage proved the reported cause wrong first: grep found only two sites that spawn the verify command, both inside worktree merge, so nothing executes it automatically. (#52) The scout was specified as 'Run status --json as the first step of EVERY session' plus a decisions re-fetch, while the preamble already renders phase, mode, feature, gates, cell and PBI counts, the critical-patterns digest and recent decisions built from the identical activeDecisions(root,{recent:3}) call — measured against inject.mjs, not assumed. (#48) No commit step existed anywhere in the close chain, so compounding-complete was set with the learnings file, decisions, backlog rows and specs uncommitted. (scratch) `bee tmp sweep --all --dry-run` reported included: 18 against a scratch home holding 76 entries — all 18 directories, and 58 loose root files invisible to EVERY flag including --all, the flag documented as clearing the lot.",
  "the_self_inflicted_trap": "The scratch finding is bee contradicting itself. Its own write guard REFUSES scratch-shaped writes elsewhere and tells the agent to 'write it to .bee/tmp/ instead ... and let bee tmp sweep clear it later' — naming the ROOT. The sweeper's entry lister then filtered to directories and symlinks only, so every file written where the guard sent it was unsweepable. The guard directed writes to precisely the place the sweeper could not reach.",
  "green_evidence": "Re-run independently by the orchestrator after the worker reported. `tmp sweep --all --dry-run` now reports files_freed 186 (was 114) and reaches the loose root entries; `--feature f3-3` now lists nine artifacts where it previously listed none. Instruction fence PASS with 0 unbranched misroutes. AGENTS.md and its template byte-consistent (test_agents_budget 4 passed). Chain 82 suites, exit 0, both with and without the agent-name prefix.",
  "51_fix": "Removed from the Startup list, remaining items renumbered including the list's own 'steps 1-7 above' self-reference, and re-landed as a critical rule in the execution section so structure and meaning agree. All four locations lead with the trigger. The gate's strength is untouched — 'never build on red' and the fix-first tiny cell survive verbatim in every copy, and one location says so explicitly: 'the gate's strength is unchanged. What changed is when it fires.'",
  "52_fix": "No call deleted; each is now conditional on intent and names the surface that already answered it. The re-fetch fires 'when you are about to ROUTE WORK — claim, plan, or change phase — or when no preamble arrived, or it went stale after a compaction', with the sharp line 'Answering a question is not routing work.' The scout spec also states what a re-run genuinely ADDS (reservations, staleness, recommended_next) rather than pretending it is redundant. Skills were not shrunk — out of scope.",
  "48_fix": "A dedicated close-commit step now sits between the scratch sweep and the state update, and the state step opens by saying the phase is set AFTER that commit lands, because compounding-complete is the claim that the close is durable. Four rules ride it: one dedicated close commit never folded into a cell commit, the feature named in the message, nothing unrelated swept in, and warn-never-block on an unresolvable refusal — with the carve-out that setting the phase while silently leaving artifacts uncommitted is never acceptable.",
  "scratch_fix": "Two changes. The entry lister returns plain files alongside directories and symlinks, with the containment proof unchanged — each candidate is still proved contained at plan time and re-proved immediately before removal. And --feature gained a prefix rule matching bee's own <feature>-<n> cell-id convention, since fixing only the lister would have left the exact command the close documents still sweeping one directory while every per-cell artifact of that feature stayed at the root. It requires a separator boundary so --feature rel1 never matches rel1100, and because a prefix match is an inference rather than the documented exact-name override, it REFUSES to eat a live sibling and reports that refusal rather than swallowing it. Five regression rows cover all of it.",
  "53_no_code_change": "Honored. Bee has no mechanism that surfaces scratch scripts; the harness displays the call like any other, and hiding it is a harness display concern outside bee. Only the adjacent stale-sweep finding was in scope, and that is what was fixed.",
  "deviation_accepted": "One scope addition, argued rather than assumed: the --feature prefix rule. The must-have was 'the documented behavior is the real behavior', and the documented per-feature sweep was the failing one — fixing only the lister would have made --all honest while leaving the close's own command broken. Accepted, with its two guards.",
  "honest_limits_reported": "The worker reported three things rather than hiding them: the live scratch home was NOT actually swept (every reproduction was a dry run, because a real sweep would delete other sessions' scratch and this cell's own evidence — that belongs to the feature close); AGENTS.md remains above the 18000-byte soft warn at 19181B, but it was already above it at 18297B before this cell and the hard 20480B budget passes with headroom; and its first baseline run tripped a known self-interference race where this session's own Stop hook mutated live state mid-suite, which an immediate re-run cleared.",
  "orchestrator_review": "Both verify invocations, the instruction fence, the AGENTS byte-consistency suite and the sweep dry-run re-run independently. Flagged diagnostics investigated: the unused readJson import in test_scratch.mjs is PRE-EXISTING at HEAD, and the unused root parameter in bee.mjs is the same pre-existing one dismissed in an earlier cell. Neither introduced here."
}
